name: Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit
name_original: Étang de Biscarrosse et de Parentis - boucle
description: This moderate 66.6-mile (107.2 km) road cycling circuit through the Haute-Landes and Pays de Born offers diverse scenery and well-maintained
region_intro: Road cycling around Mimizan is characterized by its diverse and well-preserved natural landscapes, situated between the Atlantic Ocean, the vast Landes forest, and serene lakes. The region offers an extensive network of safe bike paths, totaling 60 km, allowing cyclists to explore varied scenic settings. Terrain is generally flat, with some routes offering moderate elevation gains, making it suitable for various fitness levels. Mimizan is integrated into the Vélodyssée, providing tranquil stretches through pine forests and along the coast. Best road cycling routes around Mimizan The most popular road cycling route is Little tour south of Mimizan, an easy 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete, exploring the local landscape south of the town. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit, a moderate 66.6 miles (107.2 km) path. This route traverses the diverse landscapes of the Haute-Landes and Pays de Born regions. Local road cyclists also love the Towards the Contis-Plage lighthouse – Contis Beach loop from Mimizan, a 28.7 miles (46.3 km) trail leading towards the Contis-Plage lighthouse, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. Road cycling around Mimizan is defined by extensive pine forests, the Atlantic coastline, and the shores of Lake Aureilhan. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging loops. The routes in Mimizan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mimizan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Mimizan?
A: Mimizan offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with nearly 50 different tours available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Mimizan?
A: The terrain around Mimizan is generally flat, making it accessible for many cyclists. However, some routes do feature moderate elevation gains, particularly those that venture further into the Landes forest. You'll primarily cycle through vast pine forests, along the shores of Lake Aureilhan, and near the Atlantic coastline.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Mimizan?
A: Yes, Mimizan is well-suited for beginners and families, with 28 easy routes available. The region boasts an extensive network of safe bike paths, totaling 60 km, perfect for relaxed rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964376166/little-tour-south-of-mimizan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Little tour south of Mimizan&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 30.6 km route exploring the local landscape.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Mimizan area?
A: For more experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Mimizan offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938737630/tour-in-the-pine-forests-south-of-mimizan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour in the pine forests south of Mimizan&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 125.5 km ride with significant elevation, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938739485/haute-landes-and-pays-de-born-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit&lt;/a&gt; covers 107.2 km through diverse landscapes.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while cycling around Mimizan?
A: Mimizan's routes offer views of diverse natural features. You can cycle through the immense Landes forest with its maritime pines, along the serene shores of Lake Aureilhan, and past the extensive sandy beaches of the Atlantic coast. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/665932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contis Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, which can be reached on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35704911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Towards the Contis-Plage lighthouse – Contis Beach loop from Mimizan&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3757562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge to Flower Island&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Mimizan part of the Vélodyssée cycling route?
A: Yes, Mimizan is integrated into the Vélodyssée, a prominent Atlantic cycling route. This allows cyclists to enjoy tranquil stretches through the pine forests and along the coast, connecting to a wider network of paths.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Mimizan?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Mimizan are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35704911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Towards the Contis-Plage lighthouse – Contis Beach loop from Mimizan&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38710334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Aureilhan – Beach Bridge loop from Mimizan&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Mimizan?
A: The road cycling routes in Mimizan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the well-preserved natural landscapes, the extensive network of safe bike paths, and the diverse scenery ranging from pine forests to lake shores and the Atlantic coast.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Mimizan?
A: Mimizan, being a popular cycling destination, offers various parking options. You can typically find parking near the town center or at designated access points for major cycling paths. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations and availability, especially during peak season.

Q: Can I rent a road bike in Mimizan?
A: Yes, Mimizan has several establishments offering bike rentals, including road bikes. Look for 'Accueil Vélo' labeled services, which indicate cyclist-friendly accommodations and services, often including bike rental facilities. This makes it convenient to explore the region even if you don't bring your own bike.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Given Mimizan's popularity with cyclists, you'll find cafes and refreshment stops, particularly in and around the main town and along more frequented routes. The 'Accueil Vélo' label also signifies establishments that cater to cyclists, often providing water, snacks, and other services.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mimizan?
A: Mimizan is enjoyable for road cycling across various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer, with the shaded forest paths providing relief. The region's mild climate generally allows for cycling for much of the year.

faqs.question: Is the Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circuit is largely suitable for beginners and cyclists of varying fitness levels. The terrain is predominantly flat, with many sections utilizing well-maintained cycle paths built on old railway lines. While the tour is rated as moderate due to its distance of over 100 km, the lack of significant climbs makes it accessible for those looking for a longer, yet gentle, ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The circuit offers diverse and picturesque scenery. You'll cycle through vast pine forests, enjoy refreshing coastal paths with views of dunes and the Atlantic Ocean, and pass by tranquil lakes such as the Étang de Biscarrosse et de Parentis. The route also takes you through charming local villages, providing a varied visual experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Landes region is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. Spring (April-May) offers mild temperatures and blooming nature, while early autumn (September-October) provides comfortable weather and fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, but the extensive pine forests offer welcome shade, and coastal sections provide a refreshing breeze.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or fees for cycling within the Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling on public paths within the Parc Naturel Régional des Landes de Gascogne. However, it's important to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment, especially regarding litter and wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit?
faqs.answer: Given the circuit's length and loop nature, you can typically find parking in many of the towns along the route, such as Biscarrosse or Parentis-en-Born. These towns often have public parking areas suitable for starting your ride. Specific parking details may vary, so checking local town websites or maps for designated parking zones is recommended.

faqs.question: Is the Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the cycle paths themselves are generally accessible, specific rules for dogs can vary. Dogs are usually allowed on a leash on most public paths. However, if you plan to stop at beaches, nature reserves, or specific attractions along the way, it's best to check their individual dog policies. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and on a leash for the safety of other cyclists and wildlife.

faqs.question: What are some notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the beautiful forests and charming villages, key highlights include the serene Étang de Biscarrosse et de Parentis, perfect for a lakeside break. You can also enjoy a stunning View of the Atlantic Ocean from the Biscarrosse Dunes, offering a refreshing change of scenery from the inland routes.

faqs.question: Does this circuit connect with other well-known cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit utilizes sections of several other established cycling routes. Notably, it incorporates parts of the C1 - Circuit de la Haute-Landes and C2 - En pays de Born. You may also find it intersects with segments of major European routes like the Vélodyssée, especially along the coastal areas, and the Scandibérique, which are known for their well-maintained and often car-free paths.

faqs.question: Are there places to rent bikes or get repairs along the circuit?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Landes department is very cycling-friendly. You'll find numerous services catering to cyclists, including bike rental shops in towns like Biscarrosse and Mimizan. Many accommodations within 5 km of the cycle paths also offer bike-friendly services, and you can often find repair kits or basic repair services in larger towns along the route.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or picnic spots available on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the circuit passes through several charming villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. Additionally, many routes in the region feature designated picnic areas, allowing you to take a break and enjoy your surroundings amidst nature. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially for longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: Is the Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit a loop or an out-and-back trail?
faqs.answer: The Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit is designed as a loop, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without retracing your steps. This makes it convenient for exploring the diverse landscapes of the region in a continuous journey.
